pieces.name: aiprise
pieces.description: KYC, KYB, and fraud prevention platform — verify user identities, onboard businesses, run AML/sanctions checks, and detect fraud signals.

run_user_verification_profile.name: run_user_verification_profile
run_user_verification_profile.description: Kicks off a new identity check for a person. AiPrise will run the checks defined in your chosen template (e.g. ID document scan, liveness, address).

get_verification_url.name: get_verification_url
get_verification_url.description: Generates a secure link you can send to a person so they can complete their identity verification on an AiPrise-hosted page — no extra development needed.

get_verification_result.name: get_verification_result
get_verification_result.description: Fetches the current status and outcome of a person's identity check — whether it is still in progress, approved, or declined.

get_user_input_data.name: get_user_input_data
get_user_input_data.description: Returns the information the person entered and the documents they uploaded during their identity check (e.g. name, date of birth, ID document details).

update_verification_result.name: update_verification_result
update_verification_result.description: Manually changes the outcome of an identity check — useful when a human reviewer needs to approve or decline a case that AiPrise flagged for manual review.

run_business_verification.name: run_business_verification
run_business_verification.description: Kicks off a company background check. AiPrise will run the checks defined in your template — which can include company registry lookup, ownership structure (UBO), sanctions screening, and adverse media.

get_business_verification_result.name: get_business_verification_result
get_business_verification_result.description: Fetches the current status and outcome of a company's background check — whether it is still running, approved, or declined.

get_business_input_data.name: get_business_input_data
get_business_input_data.description: Returns the company information provided during a business verification — such as company name, registration number, directors, and ownership details.

run_document_check.name: run_document_check
run_document_check.description: Runs a verification check on a document belonging to an existing business profile. AiPrise will process the uploaded file against the checks defined in the chosen template.

create_business_profile.name: create_business_profile
create_business_profile.description: Creates a new business profile in AiPrise. The returned profile can then be used to run document checks or other business verifications.

create_user_profile.name: create_user_profile
create_user_profile.description: Creates a new user profile in AiPrise. The returned profile can then be used for repeated verifications or ongoing monitoring for the same person.

get_user_profile.name: get_user_profile
get_user_profile.description: Fetches a user profile from AiPrise by its ID — including the person's stored details, tags, linked verification sessions, and metadata.

get_business_profile.name: get_business_profile
get_business_profile.description: Fetches a business profile from AiPrise by its ID — including the stored company details, addresses, tags, linked verification sessions, and metadata.

search_businesses.name: search_businesses
search_businesses.description: Searches public business registries by name and country, returning a list of matching companies with their entity IDs, types, and addresses. Useful for looking up a business before creating a profile or running a verification.

get_additional_user_info.name: get_additional_user_info
get_additional_user_info.description: Fetches supplementary information collected from the person during a verification — anything beyond the primary identity fields, such as follow-up answers, extra documents, or custom form data.

get_business_documents.name: get_business_documents
get_business_documents.description: Lists all documents attached to a business profile — including file UUIDs, names, S3 URLs, timestamps, document sources, and associated verification sessions.

retrieve_memories.description: Perform similarity-based semantic retrieval across one or more spaces. Returns matching chunks ranked by relevance, with optional full memory definitions.

pieces.name: google-bigquery
pieces.description: Query, analyze, and stream data into Google BigQuery — the fully managed, serverless data warehouse

run_query.name: run_query
run_query.description: Execute a SQL query on BigQuery and return the results as flat rows

create_row.name: create_row
create_row.description: Creates a single new row in a BigQuery table. Column fields are loaded from the table schema.

create_rows.name: create_rows
create_rows.description: Creates new rows of data in a BigQuery table (accepts an array of row objects). Rows are available to query within seconds.

delete_rows.name: delete_rows
delete_rows.description: Deletes one or more rows from a BigQuery table using a SQL WHERE condition.

update_rows.name: update_rows
update_rows.description: Updates one or more existing rows in a BigQuery table using SQL SET and WHERE expressions.

find_one_row.name: find_one_row
find_one_row.description: Find a single row by specifying a WHERE clause and an optional ORDER BY. Returns the first matching row, or empty if none found.

find_or_create_row.name: find_or_create_row
find_or_create_row.description: Searches for a row matching a WHERE clause. If found, returns it. If not found, inserts the provided row data and returns that.

get_rows_for_job.name: get_rows_for_job
get_rows_for_job.description: Retrieves the result rows from a completed BigQuery query job by Job ID. Use this after a "Query Job Completed" trigger to fetch the full result set.

import_data.name: import_data
import_data.description: Imports a batch of rows into a BigQuery table. Accepts a JSON array or newline-delimited JSON (NDJSON). Large datasets are automatically split into chunks.

custom_api_call.name: custom_api_call
custom_api_call.description: Make a custom API call to a specific endpoint

new_row.name: new_row
new_row.description: Triggers when a new row is added to a BigQuery table. Polls every 5 minutes by comparing the latest value in a sort column against the previous check.

updated_row.name: updated_row
updated_row.description: Triggers when an existing row is updated in a BigQuery table. Requires an `updated_at` TIMESTAMP column that is set whenever a row changes.

query_job_completed.name: query_job_completed
query_job_completed.description: Triggers when a BigQuery query job finishes successfully. Each flow run receives the job metadata and the result rows from that query.

new_job_completed.name: new_job_completed
new_job_completed.description: Triggers when any BigQuery job (query, load, copy, or extract) finishes. Returns the job metadata including status, type, and timing.

search_buckets.description: Search buckets by name. Perfect for finding buckets in your project.

delete_object_acl.description: Remove an ACL entry from an object. Perfect for revoking access for a user.

delete_bucket_acl.name: delete_bucket_acl
delete_bucket_acl.description: Remove an ACL entry from a bucket. Perfect for revoking access for a user or group.

create_bucket_default_object_acl.name: create_bucket_default_object_acl
create_bucket_default_object_acl.description: Set default ACLs for new objects added to a bucket. Perfect for automatically assigning permissions to new uploads.

delete_bucket_default_object_acl.name: delete_bucket_default_object_acl
delete_bucket_default_object_acl.description: Remove default ACL settings from a bucket. Perfect for reverting to default behavior.

new_object_created.description: Triggers when a new object is created in a bucket

object_updated.description: Triggers when an existing object is updated in a bucket

pieces.name: qawafel
pieces.description: B2B marketplace and ZATCA-compliant document platform — sync products, orders, merchants and invoices with Saudi Arabia's leading wholesale network.

create_product.name: create_product
create_product.description: Add a new product to your Qawafel catalog. The workhorse for catalog sync from your ERP or storefront into Qawafel.

update_product.name: update_product
update_product.description: Edit a product's price, description, or active state. Only the fields you fill in are updated — leave the rest blank to keep them unchanged.

get_product.name: get_product
get_product.description: Fetch a single product by its Qawafel ID. Returns the full product including price, descriptions, and active status.

list_products.name: list_products
list_products.description: Get products from your catalog, with optional filters by type, supplier, SKU, and active status. Returns up to 500 products (5 pages of 100).

create_order.name: create_order
create_order.description: Sync a completed (fulfilled) order into Qawafel. Use this to import historical or externally-completed orders from another storefront, ERP, or B2B portal — the order is created directly in **Fulfilled** state and skips the normal fulfilment workflow. Do **not** use this for new orders that still need to be confirmed, picked, and delivered.

update_order_status.name: update_order_status
update_order_status.description: Move an order forward in the Qawafel fulfilment workflow. Qawafel enforces a strict state machine — for example, an order must be **Out for Delivery** before it can be marked **Delivered**. To cancel an order, use the **Cancel Order** action instead.

cancel_order.name: cancel_order
cancel_order.description: Cancel an open order. Once cancelled, the order cannot be reopened — the only path forward is to create a new order.

get_order.name: get_order
get_order.description: Fetch a single order by its Qawafel ID. Returns the full order including line items, totals, delivery details, and current state.

list_orders.name: list_orders
list_orders.description: Get orders, optionally filtered by status, customer, or creation date. Returns up to 500 orders (5 pages of 100).

create_merchant.name: create_merchant
create_merchant.description: Add a new customer or supplier to your Qawafel tenant. Use this when onboarding a new B2B partner from your CRM, signup form, or another system.

update_merchant.name: update_merchant
update_merchant.description: Edit a merchant's trade name or active state. Only the fields you fill in are updated. (Legal name, CR, VAT and other identity fields cannot be changed via the API.)

create_invoice.name: create_invoice
create_invoice.description: Create an invoice in Qawafel for a customer. The invoice starts in `draft` state — use the **Generate Invoice** action (or call it from your dashboard) to finalize and issue a ZATCA-compliant copy.

get_invoice.name: get_invoice
get_invoice.description: Fetch a single invoice by its Qawafel ID. Returns the full invoice including line items, totals, ZATCA PDF URL, and current state.

list_invoices.name: list_invoices
list_invoices.description: Get invoices, optionally filtered by status, customer, or creation date. Returns up to 500 invoices (5 pages of 100).

custom_api_call.name: custom_api_call
custom_api_call.description: Make a custom API call to a specific endpoint

new_order.name: new_order
new_order.description: Fires the moment a new sales order is created in Qawafel. Use it to fan out to fulfillment, accounting, Slack alerts, or your CRM.

order_status_changed.name: order_status_changed
order_status_changed.description: Fires when an order moves to a specific status (confirmed, out for delivery, delivered, fulfilled, cancelled, etc.). Pick one status per trigger — add the trigger again for additional statuses.

invoice_paid.name: invoice_paid
invoice_paid.description: Fires when an invoice is marked as paid in Qawafel. Useful for posting payment receipts to Slack, releasing fulfilment, or syncing revenue to your books.

new_invoice.name: new_invoice
new_invoice.description: Fires when a ZATCA-compliant invoice is finalized in Qawafel (the `invoice.generated` event). Use it to email PDFs to customers, archive invoices to Drive, or push to your accounting system.

new_product.name: new_product
new_product.description: Fires when a new product is created in Qawafel. Use it to mirror your catalog into Shopify, Salla, Zid, or your data warehouse.

product_updated.name: product_updated
product_updated.description: Fires when a product is updated (price change, description edit, activated/deactivated). Use it to keep external storefronts in sync.

new_merchant.name: new_merchant
new_merchant.description: Fires when a new merchant (customer or supplier) is created in Qawafel. Use it to onboard customers in your CRM or add suppliers to your procurement system.

refund_requested.name: refund_requested
refund_requested.description: Fires when a credit note is created against an invoice (i.e. a customer return or refund is initiated). Use it to alert your finance team or trigger a return workflow.

expire-short-link.name: expire-short-link

get-domain-statistics.name: get-domain-statistics

get-short-link-info-by-path.name: get-short-link-info-by-path

list-short-links.name: list-short-links

update-short-link.name: update-short-link
update-short-link.description: Update an existing short link's original URL, path, title, or other properties using its link ID.

pieces.description: Connect with SimpliRoute, the last-mile delivery optimization platform. Manage clients, vehicles, visits, routes, and optimize your delivery operations.

get_me.name: get_me
get_me.description: Returns account information of the authenticated user via API token.

get_clients.name: get_clients
get_clients.description: Retrieves the list of clients associated with the account. Can filter by client key.

create_clients.name: create_clients
create_clients.description: Create one or multiple new clients in the account.

bulk_delete_clients.name: bulk_delete_clients
bulk_delete_clients.description: Delete multiple clients in a single call.

create_client_property.name: create_client_property
create_client_property.description: Create a new custom attribute for clients.

get_vehicles.name: get_vehicles
get_vehicles.description: Retrieve the list of vehicles registered in the account.

create_vehicle.name: create_vehicle
create_vehicle.description: Register a new vehicle in the account.

get_vehicle.name: get_vehicle
get_vehicle.description: Retrieve information of a specific vehicle.

delete_vehicle.name: delete_vehicle

get_visits.name: get_visits
get_visits.description: Retrieve all registered visits. Can be filtered by planned visit date.

create_visits.name: create_visits

get_visit.name: get_visit
get_visit.description: Retrieve details of a specific visit by ID.

update_visit_partial.name: update_visit_partial
update_visit_partial.description: Partially update fields of an existing visit.

update_visit.name: update_visit
update_visit.description: Completely update an existing visit with all fields.

delete_visit.name: delete_visit

add_visit_items.name: add_visit_items
add_visit_items.description: Add items to an existing visit.

get_routes.name: get_routes
get_routes.description: Retrieve the list of existing routes.

create_route.name: create_route

get_route.name: get_route
get_route.description: Retrieve details of a specific route.

delete_route.name: delete_route
delete_route.description: Delete a route by its ID.

get_plans.name: get_plans
get_plans.description: Retrieve the list of saved routing plans (executions).

create_plan.name: create_plan

get_plan_vehicles.name: get_plan_vehicles
get_plan_vehicles.displayName: Get vehicles with routes on date
get_plan_vehicles.description: Returns the vehicles that have planned routes on the indicated date.

get_visit_detail.name: get_visit_detail
get_visit_detail.description: Get detailed information about a specific visit.

get_drivers.name: get_drivers
get_drivers.description: Retrieve the list of drivers registered in the account.

create_users.name: create_users
create_users.description: Create new user/driver in the account.

get_user.name: get_user
get_user.description: Retrieve information of a specific user by ID.

update_user.name: update_user
update_user.description: Update information of an existing user.

get_skills.name: get_skills
get_skills.description: Retrieve the list of skills available in the account.

get_observations.name: get_observations
get_observations.description: Retrieve the list of observations available in the account.

get_tags.name: get_tags
get_tags.description: Retrieve the list of tags available in the account.

get_zones.name: get_zones
get_zones.description: Retrieve the list of zones available in the account.

get_fleets.name: get_fleets
get_fleets.description: Retrieve the list of fleets available in the account.

get_sellers.name: get_sellers
get_sellers.description: Retrieve the list of sellers available in the account.

create_booking.description: Create a new booking using the book API method. Returns appointment info or throws exception if time not available.

create_booking_comment.name: create_booking_comment
create_booking_comment.description: Set a comment for a booking

create_client.name: create_client
create_client.description: Add a new client with specified data. Email, phone, or both may be required (check company settings).

create_note.name: create_note
create_note.description: Create a note (generic) in the system

create_detailed_report.name: create_detailed_report
create_detailed_report.description: Generate a detailed report (metrics, bookings, revenue)

cancel_booking.name: cancel_booking
cancel_booking.description: Cancel an existing booking. Returns true on success.

find_booking.name: find_booking
find_booking.description: Returns list of bookings filtered by given parameters

find_client.name: find_client
find_client.description: Returns list of clients associated with company. Search by phone number, email address, or name.

find_invoice.name: find_invoice
find_invoice.description: Find invoices with filters and pagination

new_booking.name: new_booking
new_booking.description: Triggers when a new booking is created in SimplyBook.me

booking_changed.name: booking_changed
booking_changed.description: Triggers when a booking is changed in SimplyBook.me

booking_canceled.name: booking_canceled
booking_canceled.description: Triggers when a booking is canceled in SimplyBook.me

new_client.name: new_client
new_client.description: Triggers when a new client is added (via booking or manually) in SimplyBook.me

new_invoice.name: new_invoice
new_invoice.description: Triggers when a new invoice is generated/paid in SimplyBook.me (requires Accept Payments feature)

new_offer.name: new_offer
new_offer.description: Triggers when a new offer (proposal or quote) is created (bookings with promo: false)

pieces.name: simplyprint
pieces.description: 3D printer fleet management: monitor printers, manage the print queue, track filament, and automate print jobs.

list_printers.name: list_printers
list_printers.description: List every printer in your SimplyPrint account with current status.

get_farm_overview.name: get_farm_overview
get_farm_overview.description: One-shot fleet summary: total printer count plus per-bucket lists (online/offline, printing/paused, awaiting bed clear, in maintenance, requires-attention, AI failure detections, …). Use this instead of paginating "List Printers" when you just need counts and the printers in each state.

get_printer.name: get_printer
get_printer.description: Get detailed information about a specific printer.

pause_print.name: pause_print
pause_print.description: Pause the current print on a printer.

resume_print.name: resume_print
resume_print.description: Resume a paused print on a printer.

cancel_print.name: cancel_print
cancel_print.description: Cancel the current print on a printer. This is destructive — the job cannot be resumed.

cancel_pending_print.name: cancel_pending_print
cancel_pending_print.description: Cancel a print that's queued/staged on a printer but hasn't started yet (status = print_pending). Distinct from "Cancel Print", which targets the active print.

clear_printer_bed.name: clear_printer_bed
clear_printer_bed.description: Mark a printer's bed as cleared after a print finishes. Bread-and-butter step in AutoPrint flows — pair with a "print done" trigger to automate bed clearing.

skip_print_objects.name: skip_print_objects
skip_print_objects.description: Skip one or more individual model objects on the current print (printer must be in PRINTING / PAUSED / PAUSING / CANCELLING / RESUMING state). Single printer per call.

set_printer_out_of_order.name: set_printer_out_of_order
set_printer_out_of_order.description: Mark one or more printers as out-of-order (or clear that flag). Requires the Out-of-Order feature on the account.

send_gcode.name: send_gcode
send_gcode.description: Send raw G-code commands to an operational printer (requires Print Farm plan).

start_print.name: start_print
start_print.description: Start a print job on one or more printers from an existing file, queue item, or print-job reference.

get_printer_notifications.name: get_printer_notifications
get_printer_notifications.description: Fetch the open notifications/events on one or more printers. Pairs well with the `requires_attention` bucket from "Get Farm Overview".

resolve_printer_notification.name: resolve_printer_notification
resolve_printer_notification.description: Mark a printer notification as resolved (or dismissed). For notifications with multiple actions, pass `action` to pick one or `force: true` to resolve without choosing.

list_queue.name: list_queue
list_queue.description: List items in the print queue, optionally filtered to a single queue group.

get_queue_item.name: get_queue_item
get_queue_item.description: Fetch a single queue item by numeric ID — returns the formatted record (file metadata, assignments, custom fields, …).

get_next_queue_items.name: get_next_queue_items
get_next_queue_items.description: Resolve the next-up queue items for one or more printers using the company's match criteria (with deduplication so the same item is never assigned twice). Returns the matched queue object with item references per printer.

get_next_queue_items_for_printers.name: get_next_queue_items_for_printers
get_next_queue_items_for_printers.displayName: Preview Next Queue Items per Printer
get_next_queue_items_for_printers.description: Read-only preview of the next-up queue item for each given printer (no dedup, no offline checks). For each printer, returns either the matched queue item or the issues blocking a match. Different from "Get Next Queue Items": that one dedupes for actual scheduling, this one shows what each printer would pick.

list_queue_groups.name: list_queue_groups
list_queue_groups.description: List every queue group on your account.

save_queue_group.name: save_queue_group
save_queue_group.description: Create or update a queue group. Pass an existing group ID to edit it; omit it to create a new one. Requires the Queue Groups feature on the account.

delete_queue_group.name: delete_queue_group
delete_queue_group.description: Delete a queue group. Items in the group are moved to "Move to" group, or to the first remaining group if not specified, or set to ungrouped if it was the last group.

add_to_queue.name: add_to_queue
add_to_queue.description: Add an existing file (API-uploaded or user-file) to the print queue. For uploading a new file at the same time, use "Upload File & Add to Queue".

update_queue_item.name: update_queue_item
update_queue_item.description: Update a queue item (amount, note, etc.).

set_queue_item_printers.name: set_queue_item_printers
set_queue_item_printers.description: Assign one or more queue items to specific printers, printer models, and/or printer groups. Replaces the current assignments on each item.

move_queue_item.name: move_queue_item
move_queue_item.description: Move a queue item to a different queue group.

remove_from_queue.name: remove_from_queue
remove_from_queue.description: Remove an item from the print queue. Destructive — the item is gone.

revive_queue_item.name: revive_queue_item
revive_queue_item.description: Bring a completed (done) queue item back to the active queue.

empty_queue.name: empty_queue
empty_queue.description: Delete every item from the print queue (optionally filtered to a group or done-only). Destructive.

list_pending_queue_items.name: list_pending_queue_items
list_pending_queue_items.description: List queue items awaiting approval, denied, or sent back for revision.

approve_queue_item.name: approve_queue_item
approve_queue_item.description: Approve one or more pending queue items.

deny_queue_item.name: deny_queue_item
deny_queue_item.description: Deny a pending queue item (remove or send back for revision).

list_files.name: list_files
list_files.description: List files in your SimplyPrint account, optionally within a folder and/or filtered by a search term.

upload_file.name: upload_file
upload_file.description: Upload a file or HTTPS URL to SimplyPrint. URL uploads stream with ~95 MiB peak RAM; File uploads are buffered by AP. Files over 95 MiB are chunked automatically. Returns the API file id — pass it to "Add File to Queue" or "Start Print" later.

upload_and_queue.name: upload_and_queue
upload_and_queue.description: Upload a local file or HTTPS URL to SimplyPrint and add it to the print queue in one step. URL uploads stream with ~95 MiB peak RAM; File uploads are buffered by AP. Files over 95 MiB are chunked automatically. For starting the print afterwards, chain a "Start Print" action.

upload_to_folder.name: upload_to_folder
upload_to_folder.description: Upload a file (or HTTPS URL) and save it to your SimplyPrint user file system, optionally inside a specific folder. URL uploads stream with ~95 MiB peak RAM; File uploads are buffered by AP. Files over 95 MiB are chunked automatically.

move_file.name: move_file
move_file.description: Move one or more user files to a different folder.

create_folder.name: create_folder
create_folder.description: Create a new folder in the user file system, or update an existing one (pass the folder ID to edit). Optionally an "organization" / shared folder with per-rank view/upload/modify permissions.

delete_files.name: delete_files
delete_files.description: Permanently delete one or more user files by UID. Returns a partial-success report — files the caller can't modify are reported in `errors` while accessible files are deleted.

delete_folders.name: delete_folders
delete_folders.description: Delete one or more folders (and everything in them). All-or-nothing: if any folder isn't modifiable by the caller, the whole call fails.

update_file.name: update_file
update_file.description: Rename a user file and / or update its compatible-printer assignments. (Thumbnail upload is not exposed here — multipart isn't supported on this endpoint over OAuth via the AP HTTP client.)

download_file.name: download_file
download_file.description: Download a file from SimplyPrint by user-file UID, queue-item ID, or print-job UID. Returns the file as an Activepieces file reference plus content-type / size metadata.

list_filaments.name: list_filaments
list_filaments.description: List filament spools in your SimplyPrint account, with optional filters and sort.

get_filament.name: get_filament
get_filament.description: Get detailed information about a specific filament spool by numeric ID or 4-character short ID (uid).

create_filament.name: create_filament
create_filament.description: Add a new filament spool to your inventory. Use the free-text brand/color fields for ad-hoc spools or the catalog ids for known SKUs.

adjust_filament_weight.name: adjust_filament_weight
adjust_filament_weight.description: Update the remaining filament on a spool. Provide ONE of "grams remaining" / "percent remaining" / "weighed gross" — the other two derive from it.

mark_filament_dried.name: mark_filament_dried
mark_filament_dried.description: Record that one or more spools were just dried. Updates each spool's last-dried timestamp.

get_filament_history.name: get_filament_history
get_filament_history.description: Paginated usage history for a specific spool — print jobs, weight adjustments, drying cycles, etc.

assign_filament.name: assign_filament
assign_filament.description: Assign a filament spool to a printer at a specific nozzle + extruder slot. Defaults to nozzle 0 / extruder 0 (single-nozzle single-extruder printers); set the indices for multi-nozzle (e.g. IDEX, toolchanger) or multi-material (MMS, MMU) printers.

unassign_filament.name: unassign_filament
unassign_filament.description: Detach a specific filament spool from whichever printer it is currently assigned to.

list_print_history.name: list_print_history
list_print_history.description: List completed print jobs, optionally filtered to a single printer.

get_print_job.name: get_print_job
get_print_job.description: Fetch a single print job's full record by its UID — timeline, pictures, spools used, gcode analysis, filament data, cost, AI inference, custom fields, etc.

archive_print_jobs.name: archive_print_jobs
archive_print_jobs.description: Archive one or more completed print jobs (removes them from the active history view).

unarchive_print_jobs.name: unarchive_print_jobs
unarchive_print_jobs.description: Restore one or more archived print jobs to the active history view.

list_tags.name: list_tags
list_tags.description: List all custom tags configured on your account.

create_tag.name: create_tag
create_tag.description: Create a new custom tag, or rename / re-style an existing one (pass the tag ID to edit).

assign_tag.name: assign_tag
assign_tag.description: Attach an existing custom tag to a printer, printer group, file, or queue item. Use "Create or Update Tag" first to get the tag ID.

detach_tag.name: detach_tag
detach_tag.description: Remove a single custom tag from one printer, printer group, file, or queue item.

delete_tag.name: delete_tag
delete_tag.description: Delete a custom tag from the account. The tag is removed from every printer/file/queue item it was attached to.

get_current_user.name: get_current_user
get_current_user.description: Return information about the SimplyPrint account connected to this flow.

list_custom_fields.name: list_custom_fields
list_custom_fields.description: List all custom field definitions on your account.

set_custom_field_values.name: set_custom_field_values
set_custom_field_values.description: Set custom field values on one or more entities (queue items, files, printers, etc.).

get_statistics.name: get_statistics
get_statistics.description: Fetch high-level printing statistics (total prints, materials used, hours, etc.).

custom_api_call.name: custom_api_call
custom_api_call.description: Call any SimplyPrint REST endpoint the piece does not wrap directly. Useful for admin / partner / school-dashboard endpoints and anything else behind OAuth scopes.

print_started.name: print_started
print_started.description: Fires when a print job starts on any printer.

print_paused.name: print_paused
print_paused.description: Fires when a print is paused (manually or by the AI failure watcher).

print_resumed.name: print_resumed
print_resumed.description: Fires when a paused print is resumed.

print_cancelled.name: print_cancelled
print_cancelled.description: Fires when a print is cancelled before finishing.

print_finished.name: print_finished
print_finished.description: Fires when a print completes successfully.

print_failed.name: print_failed
print_failed.description: Fires when a print terminates with a failure.

bed_cleared.name: bed_cleared
bed_cleared.description: Fires when a printer's bed is reported cleared (ready for the next job).

objects_skipped.name: objects_skipped
objects_skipped.description: Fires when one or more objects in a multi-object print are skipped.

printer_autoprint_state_changed.name: printer_autoprint_state_changed
printer_autoprint_state_changed.description: Fires when a printer's AutoPrint enabled/disabled state changes.

printer_nozzle_size_changed.name: printer_nozzle_size_changed
printer_nozzle_size_changed.description: Fires when a printer's active nozzle diameter is updated.

printer_material_changed.name: printer_material_changed
printer_material_changed.description: Fires when a printer's loaded material is updated.

printer_tag_assigned.name: printer_tag_assigned
printer_tag_assigned.description: Fires when a custom tag is attached to a printer.

get_app_info.name: get_app_info
get_app_info.description: Get information about the partner application associated with your token — title, description, domain, logo URL, public key, and active flag. Useful for verifying your app configuration.

check_company_paths.name: check_company_paths
check_company_paths.description: Quickly check if you have connections to a company without fetching full details. Returns whether paths exist, an overall score, count of reachable people, and avatar thumbnails.

companies_refresh.name: companies_refresh
companies_refresh.description: Refresh/import company data from LinkedIn URLs or domains. Realtime mode returns enriched data synchronously (or times out after 25s); async mode returns job IDs for later status checking. At least one of linkedin_urls or domains must be provided.

enrich_company.name: enrich_company
enrich_company.description: Get detailed information about a company by domain, LinkedIn URL, or any URL. Returns name, website, LinkedIn URL, logo, employee count, industry, founded year, and description.

enrich_company_bulk.name: enrich_company_bulk
enrich_company_bulk.description: Get detailed information for up to 10 companies in a single request. Each result contains either company data or an error.

get_company_paths.name: get_company_paths
get_company_paths.description: Find introduction paths to people at a specific company. Returns up to 50 employees you can reach through your network, ranked by connection strength.

list_companies.name: list_companies
list_companies.description: Get your top-connected companies ranked by network strength. Returns a paginated list with connection score, label, LinkedIn URL, domain, and enrichment data.

search_companies.name: search_companies
search_companies.description: Search for companies using natural language (in the "prompt" field) and/or structured filters. Returns matching companies with relevance and connection-strength scores; supports cursor-based pagination.

sort_companies.name: sort_companies
sort_companies.description: Rank a list of companies (up to 100 LinkedIn URLs or domains) by how well-connected you are to people there. Each result includes a score, label, LinkedIn URL, and domain.

join_group.name: join_group
join_group.description: Join a group using an invite link. Once joined, you gain access to the shared network of all group members.

leave_group.name: leave_group
leave_group.description: Leave a group you are a member of. If you are the last member, the group is deleted. Admins must remove all other members before leaving.

list_groups.name: list_groups
list_groups.description: Get all groups (communities) you are a member of. Groups allow members to share network access with each other, expanding everyone's reach.

upsert_group.name: upsert_group
upsert_group.description: Create a new group or update an existing one. To create: provide a name (id is auto-generated). To update: provide group_id (requires admin permissions).

list_integrations.name: list_integrations
list_integrations.description: Get all your connected Gmail integrations, including rate limits, timezone, the default sending account, and whether you can add more based on your plan.

resync_integration.name: resync_integration
resync_integration.description: Trigger a full resync of a Google or LinkedIn integration. Clears sync cursors and re-imports all contacts/calendar data; all graph operations use MERGE so it is fully idempotent.

update_integration.name: update_integration
update_integration.description: Update settings for a connected Gmail integration — adjust rate limits, timezone, status, or set as the default sending account. Provide only the fields you want to change.

add_list_items.name: add_list_items
add_list_items.description: Add people or companies to a list. Each identifier is resolved (LinkedIn URLs, company domains, emails, or graph IDs) and added. Returns detailed results per item.

check_list_membership.name: check_list_membership
check_list_membership.description: Check which lists contain the given entities. Returns a map of entity_id to the lists that contain it. Entities not in any list are omitted.

create_list.name: create_list
create_list.description: Create a new list to organize people or companies. The creator becomes the list owner. List creation may be limited based on your subscription plan.

delete_list.name: delete_list
delete_list.description: Permanently delete a list and all its items. This action cannot be undone.

get_list.name: get_list
get_list.description: Get a list with all its items. Returns the list metadata plus paginated items. For people lists, items are sorted by connection strength (warmth score).

list_lists.name: list_lists
list_lists.description: Get all your saved lists of people or companies. Supports pagination, filtering by type, and searching by title.

remove_list_items.name: remove_list_items
remove_list_items.description: Remove items from a list by their item IDs (obtained from get_list). Returns the count of successfully removed items.

update_list.name: update_list
update_list.description: Update a list's metadata. Provide at least one of: title or description. The list type cannot be changed after creation.

enrich_person.name: enrich_person
enrich_person.description: Get detailed profile information (name, headline, current company, location, photo, LinkedIn URL) for a person identified by LinkedIn URL or generic URL.

enrich_person_bulk.name: enrich_person_bulk
enrich_person_bulk.description: Get detailed profile information for up to 10 people in a single request. Partial results are returned if some lookups fail — check each result for success or error status.

enrich_person_email.name: enrich_person_email
enrich_person_email.description: Find the email address for a person by LinkedIn URL or generic URL. Returns the verified email address if found, or null if unavailable.

enrich_person_email_bulk.name: enrich_person_email_bulk
enrich_person_email_bulk.description: Find email addresses for up to 10 people in a single request. Returns email addresses where found, with error messages for failed lookups.

get_person_paths.name: get_person_paths
get_person_paths.description: Find introduction paths to reach a specific person through your professional network. Provide a LinkedIn URL and get back direct connections, mutual contacts, and connection strength scores (0-100).

get_person_paths_bulk.name: get_person_paths_bulk
get_person_paths_bulk.description: Find introduction paths to multiple people in a single request. Provide up to 100 LinkedIn IDs (the slug after linkedin.com/in/) and get connection paths for each.

list_people.name: list_people
list_people.description: Get your network contacts ranked by connection strength. Returns a paginated list with enrichment data, supporting cursor-based pagination and optional connection-degree filtering.

people_refresh.name: people_refresh
people_refresh.description: Refresh or import people data from LinkedIn URLs. In realtime mode, returns enriched person data immediately (or times out after 25 seconds and converts to async). In async mode, returns job IDs for later status checking.

search_people.name: search_people
search_people.description: Search for people using natural language queries. Pass your search text in "prompt" (e.g. "engineers at Google"). Supports cursor-based pagination and structured filters.

sort_people.name: sort_people
sort_people.description: Rank a list of LinkedIn profile URLs by how well-connected you are to them. Returns each person sorted by connection strength (highest first), with score (0-100) and score_label.

join_team.name: join_team
join_team.description: Join a team using an invite link. Provide the invite link code (the part after /join/ in the URL).

leave_team.name: leave_team
leave_team.description: Leave a team you are a member of. If you are the last member of a non-paid team, the team is deleted. Admins of paid teams cannot leave — use Cancel Plan instead.

list_teams.name: list_teams
list_teams.description: Get all teams you are a member of. Each team includes id, name, logo, invite_link, and an is_admin flag indicating your admin status.

upsert_team.name: upsert_team
upsert_team.description: Create or update a team. The team ID is derived from the team name; repeated calls with the same name update the same team (requires admin permissions on update).

get_current_user.name: get_current_user
get_current_user.description: Get profile information for the authenticated user — id, email, name, account creation date, sync completion status, and active flag. Useful for verifying auth and checking whether the user's network data is ready.

import_relationships.name: import_relationships
import_relationships.description: Import manual relationships to expand your network graph — useful for adding people you know but aren't connected to digitally. Provide email addresses or LinkedIn URLs with optional scores (0-10); higher scores indicate stronger relationships. Max 1000 per request.
